items.title: Registration
Beyond Boundaries: The Triumph of a Decoupled Web Portal for IDMC a part of the Norwegian Refugee Co
items.full_description:
The Internal Displacement Monitoring Centre (IDMC), established in 1998 as part of the Norwegian Refugee Council (NRC), is the world's leading authority on internal displacement data and analysis. IDMC commits itself to providing the international community with exacting, autonomous, and trusted insights. Their journey demonstrates the power of compassion and knowledge in the face of unprecedented worldwide disasters and uncertainty. It's a story of tenacity and resilience, illustrating the human spirit's astonishing capacity to continue shining brightly even in the darkest of times.
The three main components of this ambitious project are:
Decoupled Approach: Utilising Drupal+Gatsby to its full potential
Seamless transitioning from Drupal 9 to the most recent version of Drupal 10
Completing the entire process in an astoundingly short three months.
Find out how this groundbreaking digital transformation of a non-profit organization (NGO) sets new benchmarks for efficiency and creativity in website development and transformation, redefining how organizations approach these tasks.
Join us to explore more about the decoupled approach (Drupal and Gatsby hybrid solution)
This presentation explores the
Journey
Challenges Faced
The solution was built using React, Gatsby, and GraphQL technologies, which optimizes the site and makes it editor-friendly by allowing us to reuse components efficiently with GraphQL queries.
Key Features
Successes in developing a modern, user-centric platform to enhance data accessibility
Driving positive change for displaced populations worldwide.
Target Audience: Digital managers, Business owners, Consultants, Product owners, Project managers, Agency leaders, Tech leads, Content creators, Drupal enthusiasts & Quality Assurance Engineers
Learning Outcomes - The target audience can expect to gain insights into:
Innovative Technology: Understanding how decoupled architecture can revolutionize web development for non-profits, enabling greater flexibility and scalability.
Impactful Storytelling: Learning how the Norwegian Refugee Council's Internal Displacement Monitoring Center leveraged the portal to enhance search features and storytelling, amplifying their impact.
Best Practices: Discovering best practices and strategies for implementing a decoupled web portal, with a focus on user experience, content management, and integration.
Transformational Results: Exploring the tangible results and benefits achieved by IDMC through the implementation of the decoupled web portal, including increased engagement, efficiency, and effectiveness in humanitarian efforts.
Future Opportunities: Identifying future opportunities and considerations for non-profits looking to adopt a decoupled approach, and how it can drive digital transformation and mission success.
items.summary: The Internal Displacement Monitoring Centre (IDMC), established in 1998 as part of the Norwegian Refugee Council (NRC), is the world's leading authority on internal displacement data and analysis....

items.title: Automating Behavior-Driven Development: Boosting Productivity with Template-Driven Test Experience
items.full_description:
We have successfully established our development process for Drupal custom modules, including automated testing using PHPUnit, all managed through our own GitLab CI/CD pipeline. This setup mirrors the automated testing process used by Drupal.org, which was our goal to emulate.
Building on this success, we have taken the next step by learning Behavior-Driven Development (BDD) using Behat. This approach allows us to automate the execution of acceptance tests for our Cloud Orchestration modules. Our upcoming session will provide a thorough explanation of the practical application of Behat, demonstrating how to effectively use this tool to write and execute comprehensive test scenarios.
In this session, we will cover:
1. Introduction to Behavior-Driven Development (BDD):
- Understanding the principles of BDD and its advantages in the software development lifecycle.
- How BDD aligns with agile methodologies and enhances collaboration between developers, testers, and stakeholders.
2. Overview of Behat:
- Introduction to Behat as a testing framework for BDD.
- Key features of Behat and its integration with other tools and platforms.
3. Automating Acceptance Tests:
- Running Behat tests in our GitLab CI/CD pipeline.
- Techniques for ensuring that automated tests are reliable and maintainable.
- Strategies for continuous improvement and scaling the test suite.
4. Template-Based Test Scenario Reusability:
- How to create reusable test scenario templates in Behat.
- Methods for parameterizing test scenarios to enhance reusability and reduce redundancy.
- Practical examples of how to implement and manage these templates within your testing framework.
By the end of the session, attendees will have a comprehensive understanding of how to leverage Behat for BDD in their own projects, particularly within the context of Drupal and cloud orchestration. They will gain practical knowledge on writing and running automated acceptance tests, ultimately enhancing the quality and efficiency of their development processes.
items.summary: We have successfully established our development process for Drupal custom modules, including automated testing using PHPUnit, all managed through our own GitLab CI/CD pipeline....

items.title: Server-side rendering a Drupal site with Next.js
items.full_description:
But Drupal can already render your website on the server! Why would you want to decouple your frontend and have it render your website in JavaScript on the server?
If JavaScript developers have just discovered that rendering on the server is faster, why aren’t we just going back to Drupal’s PHP server rendering? Because it’s simpler. With React Server Components, frontend developers can leverage their knowledge of Fetch-based API integration without having to learn any PHP, Twig, Render API, library definitions, or Drupal behaviors.
React Server Components are a new feature of React v19. This session will focus on Next Drupal, a set of Drupal and NPM modules that integrates Drupal’s JSON:API or GraphQL with Next.js, an opinionated React framework. We will discuss how the differences between JavaScript's import statement and PHP's include statement affect the way we need to build and route our application. And we will get into specifics of reducing bundle size and how to weave interactive features with server-rendered content.
In this session we will cover the following topics:
* Why? Just why?
* Okay, but how?
* React Server Components in React 19 and Next.js' App Router
* Async promises on the server
* Client Components and the "use client" directive
* Suspense boundaries and React rehydration
* How the Next module improves the Drupal content editor experience
items.summary: But Drupal can already render your website on the server!...

items.title: Driesnote
Group Photo
The Path to Drupal Excellence: Delivery Success Through Professional Growth
items.full_description:
Introduction
In today's dynamic Drupal development landscape, ensuring successful project delivery is essential. Successful project delivery is often driven by highly competent and growth-oriented professionals. This session will delve into a comprehensive strategy for transforming both new-to-Drupal and existing Drupal professionals into proficient experts across various roles,. Attendees will learn proven methodologies for effective onboarding, competency development, and continuous upskilling, leading to exceptional project outcomes.
Prerequisite
Attendees will benefit most from this session if they have prior experience in Drupal project management, or team leadership, or have worked with or managed professionals in a Drupal project environment.
Outline
Entering the Drupal ecosystem can be challenging for new professionals while existing Drupal experts face the need to adapt and grow with the evolving platform. This session will explore strategies for seamlessly integrating and advancing both groups within the Drupal ecosystem amidst these challenges.
Attendees will learn about the onboarding journey and competency framework tailored for various roles within a Drupal project. By establishing clear expectations and providing targeted training, new team members can quickly become effective contributors and existing professionals can continue to excel. Continuous upskilling through Individual Development Plans (IDPs) will be discussed, highlighting how they facilitate professional growth and ensure project success.
Join this session to uncover solutions for empowering both new-to-Drupal and existing professionals to succeed in Drupal projects. Discover strategies for providing the necessary support and guidance to help all team members confidently navigate Drupal's complexities. Learn how to foster a continuous learning and improvement culture to ensure everyone is well-equipped to contribute effectively to project success and client satisfaction.
Learning Objectives
At the end of this session, attendees will be able to:
Identify the key challenges and solutions in elevating both new-to-Drupal and existing Drupal professionals for Drupal projects.
Apply practical tips and strategies for effectively onboarding and developing Drupal team members.
Utilize checklists and templates specifically designed for facilitating the onboarding and continuous development of Drupal professionals.
Develop Individual Development Plans (IDPs) to support continuous upskilling of Drupal professionals and ensure the success of Drupal projects.
items.summary: Introduction In today's dynamic Drupal development landscape, ensuring successful project delivery is essential....

items.title: Sustainable Digital Marketing with Drupal—Eco-Friendly Tech Solutions
items.full_description:
Session Description: Exploring the dynamic intersection of sustainability and digital marketing through the powerful lens of Drupal. This session is designed for marketers, developers, and business leaders eager to drive their brands toward a greener future without sacrificing technological advancement.
Session Outline:
The Importance of Sustainability in Digital Marketing
Explore the growing environmental concerns linked to the digital industry.
Discuss the rising consumer demand for eco-friendly brands and how marketers can respond.
Understanding Drupal’s Eco-Friendly Potential
Introduction to Drupal as a robust content management system.
Insights into Drupal’s sustainability initiatives and the role of open-source in promoting eco-friendly practices.
Energy-Efficient Web Design with Drupal
Learn strategies to enhance Drupal site performance, reducing energy consumption.
Best practices in efficient coding, optimizing assets, and choosing green hosting solutions.
Sustainable UX Design Principles
The critical role of UX in sustainable web design.
Practical tips for designing accessible, inclusive, and efficient user interfaces using Drupal.
Promoting Environmental Awareness through Drupal-Based Campaigns
Harness Drupal’s integration capabilities for crafting impactful, eco-conscious social media campaigns.
Examples of successful campaigns that educate and engage audiences on sustainability.
Drupal Modules and Tools for Sustainable Practices
Overview of essential Drupal tools and modules tailored for sustainable marketing.
How to utilize SEO and analytics tools to boost and measure your eco-friendly marketing efforts.
The Future of Sustainable Digital Marketing in Drupal
Upcoming trends and Drupal’s roadmap in supporting sustainable digital solutions.
Engaging with the Drupal community to foster ongoing innovation in sustainability.
Learning Objectives: Participants will leave this session equipped with the knowledge to:
Implement sustainable practices in their digital marketing efforts using Drupal.
Optimize website energy efficiency and design with user experience in mind.
Launch and manage effective environmental awareness campaigns.
Navigate and utilize Drupal’s tools for enhancing sustainability in digital operations.
Join us to pave the way for a sustainable future, leveraging the power of Drupal to make impactful changes in how digital marketing can support our planet.
items.summary: Session Description: Exploring the dynamic intersection of sustainability and digital marketing through the powerful lens of Drupal....

items.title: Building the future of Drupal: 11, 12 and Starshot
items.track: user experience, accessibility and design drupal starshot
items.full_description: As of DrupalCon Barcelona 2024, Drupal 11.0 is still very new, but we already know when Drupal 12 will be released. Work on Starshot is well underway, and the Driesnote will provide a high-level update on its progress. This session will dive deeper into how these components shape Drupal's future. How does Starshot build on Drupal core, and what does it mean for the future of core and contributed projects? What are the benefits of Starshot for new and existing sites? How can we make starting with Drupal and staying up to date easier while keeping your full control?
items.summary: As of DrupalCon Barcelona 2024, Drupal 11.0 is still very new, but we already know when Drupal 12 will be released....

items.title: Level Up Your Impact: Building Socially-Driven Projects with Drupal as a Digital Public Good
items.full_description:
=== Abstract ===
Drupal's got a social conscience! Now recognized as a Digital Public Good (DPG), Drupal empowers you to build impactful projects that address social challenges and empower communities. This session explores leveraging Drupal's features and DPG status to create impactful projects tackling real-world issues like education, citizen engagement, and environmental sustainability. We'll delve into how Drupal can empower communities and showcase how this expertise can widen your horizons, both in terms of your skillset and potential client base.
=== Outline ===
In this session, you will:
- Grasp the DPG landscape: Explore the core principles of Digital Public Goods and how Drupal aligns with this mission.
- Unlock social impact potential: Discover the advantages of using Drupal for social projects – affordability, long-term support, and scalability.
- Master Drupal's social toolkit: Delve into features that fuel social interaction and collaboration.
- Build for the future: Discuss best practices for security, content moderation, and ensuring long-term sustainability.
items.summary: === Abstract === Drupal's got a social conscience!...

items.title: Implementation of automation system in Balochistan's Public Accounts Committee
items.full_description: The Public Accounts Committee (known as PAC) of Balochistan province in Pakistan had an outdated manual system of tracking their audit cases over the years. That had caused a lot of backlog. Every year an audit book is handed over the PAC by Audit department which has audit observations of all government departments of the province. And then PAC holds meetings with representatives of that department to verify and justify public spending done by them. This whole process was manual, and on average it took 3-4 months to conduct one meeting and sometimes more. With the introduction of an automation system built in Drupal, that average timeframe per meeting was reduced to less than one month. Also, it reduced the paper usage by nearly 90% because for every meeting, there were 30+ physical prints of the documents which would be sent to all relevant departments and committee members. We have reduced that to only 2 physical prints and no other physical copies. This project was supported and approved by Accountability Lab Pakistan, and Governance and Policy Program (GPP), which works under World Bank for the digitisation of government processes.
items.summary: The Public Accounts Committee (known as PAC) of Balochistan province in Pakistan had an outdated manual system of tracking their audit cases over the years....

5 Varnish features that will improve the caching of your Drupal site

items.title: Features of Fully Open Source Enterprise Hosting
items.full_description: amazee.io has been delivering Enterprise Drupal hosting on our open source platform for over 10 years, but what does Enterprise actually mean to our customers across the globe? Hear from amazee.io about the features and solutions we’ve delivered for (and with!) our users to help manage their fleets of 10 to 1,000 business-critical sites and address essential needs such as data sovereignty and security. You’ll also get an insight into how the team works to keep them up and running, up-to-date, and performing!
items.summary: amazee.io has been delivering Enterprise Drupal hosting on our open source platform for over 10 years, but what does Enterprise actually mean to our customers across the globe?...

items.title: Adapting Hosting Platforms for Starshot’s Automatic Updates
items.full_description:
As the world of Starshot continues to take shape, its features are continuing to mature.
One such feature that has recently received attention is the ability to automatically update and browse community modules from the browser. While this may seem like a simple addition, it has far-reaching implications for hosting platforms and their operations.
Traditionally, hosting providers use processes for managing the applications they host, which can be time-consuming, error-prone, and resource-intensive without some level of automation. Starshot’s automatic update system can lift this burden for some users, but it also means that hosting platforms need to adapt their infrastructure and workflows to accommodate this new paradigm.
Attendees will leave with a deep understanding of the technical challenges hosting providers will need to overcome in the future, the safeguards currently in place, and the innovative approaches being explored to make Automatic Updates a cornerstone of Drupal's future-proof ecosystem.
This presentation is for people interested in the implications and work involved for operations teams to support Starshot in the future. This has broader impacts on the hosting ecosystem as it will provide an avenue for providers to provide a software-as-a-service option for Drupal which is an exciting prospect for individuals wanting to manage a Drupal website without all the hassle of the technical aspects.
Learning Objectives
1. A brief history of file system management in Drupal, how and why hosting providers have evolved to where they are today.
2. To understand the current state of hosting providers with regard to file system management and why things are the way they are at the moment.
3. To understand the impacts Starshot will have on hosting providers, including changes to deployment mechanisms, infrastructure differences, and growth opportunities
items.summary: As the world of Starshot continues to take shape, its features are continuing to mature....

items.title: Widening access to Justice using AI: A Case Study of some US based Legal Services Chatbot
items.full_description:
Explore how some Legal Service Organizations & QED42 leveraged the power of AI and Drupal to develop an innovative chatbot that revolutionizes access to legal information and services for the larger low-income and vulnerable populations.
We will delve into the unique challenges we faced in designing a solution appropriate for an NGO serving a huge, diverse user base with complex legal needs and how the integration of AI within their Drupal-based website proved to be a game-changer. The session will cover the critical aspects of the project, including:
- The collaborative effort between legal experts, Drupal developers, and AI specialists in designing and implementing the chatbot.
- The role of Drupal in providing a robust content management system for the chatbot's knowledge base and seamless integration with the existing website.
- The impact of the chatbot on enhancing access to justice for underserved communities and its potential for scalability and replication.
Through this case study, attendees will gain valuable insights into the practical application of AI within a Drupal-based project, the challenges and opportunities involved, and the transformative potential of technology in addressing critical social issues.
Whether you are a Drupal developer looking to explore the possibilities of AI, a content manager seeking to enhance user engagement, or a social impact organization aiming to leverage technology for good, this session will provide inspiration and actionable takeaways for your projects.
items.summary: Explore how some Legal Service Organizations & QED42 leveraged the power of AI and Drupal to develop an innovative chatbot that revolutionizes access to legal information and services for the larger low-income and vulner...

items.title: Making the Drupal Credit System Less Gamifiable
items.full_description:
Join us for an engaging panel discussion where we dive into a critical issue facing the
Drupal community: the potential for abuse of the Drupal credit system. This practice not
only undermines the integrity of the credit system but also affects the overall quality and
efficiency of our community contributions.
Our panel of experts will discuss how some contributors might be gaming the system
and explore actionable solutions to address the issue, improve the credit system and
implement improved guidelines for accumulating more credits. In this session, we will:
- Identify and categorize the types of issues being exploited for easy credit
- Evaluate limitations of the current measurement system
- Explore solutions to mitigate the gamification through automation, categorization
and an enhanced review process
- Propose updated feature and guidelines for future mitigation
This session is ideal for community members who are passionate about maintaining a
fair and effective contribution environment in Drupal. Whether you're a developer, site
builder, project manager or community leader, your insights and participation are
valuable in shaping the future of our contribution system. Let's work together to ensure
every contribution is meaningful and truly beneficial to the Drupal community.
items.summary: Join us for an engaging panel discussion where we dive into a critical issue facing the Drupal community: the potential for abuse of the Drupal credit system....

items.title: Beyond Accessibility: Crafting Empathetic Digital Experiences
items.full_description:
Ever wondered how to make your website welcoming to everyone, regardless of ability? Join me for a lightning talk where we’ll unveil a straightforward accessibility checklist that ensures your site is inclusive and user-friendly. From essential principles to actionable tips, we’ll guide you through each step, empowering you to create digital experiences that everyone can enjoy.
Have you ever felt overwhelmed by where to start on your accessibility journey? This session is crafted for developers, designers, and project managers who yearn to break down barriers and foster genuine connections in their web projects. Whether you’re building from scratch or enhancing an existing site, this checklist approach will equip you with practical tools to make a profound impact.
Target Audience:
This session is a must for anyone passionate about enhancing their website’s accessibility. Whether you’re new to accessibility or seeking to refine your approach, join me to learn practical steps that promote empathy, and usability for all users.
Outline:
- Understanding the impact of accessibility on user experience.
- A simple accessibility checklist that covers essential elements.
- Benefits of adopting a checklist for your projects.
- Practical tips and examples for implementing each item effectively.
- Lessons learned and outcomes from real projects.
items.summary: Ever wondered how to make your website welcoming to everyone, regardless of ability?...

items.title: American Diabetes Association’s Digital Landscape Overhaul with Drupal 10 & Multisite Architecture
items.full_description:
This session will delve into the comprehensive case study of the American Diabetes Association's digital landscape overhaul using Drupal 10 and multisite architecture. The discussion will cover new features and sites launched on the platform. More details can be found at https://www.drupal.org/case-study/american-diabetes-association-digital….
Key Discussion Points:
1. Customer and Project Background & Context of the industry and specific needs addressed.
2. Overview of Goals and Requirements
3. Principal Attributes of the Designed Solutions & How the solutions meet the goals and requirements of the project.
3.1. Why Drupal Multisite was Selected as a Solution - Benefits and advantages of using Drupal multisite.
3.2. Why Acquia Cloud Next was Used for Hosting - Rationale behind using Acquia Cloud Next for hosting the multi-site solution.
3.3. Dynamic Layout Building Experience- Component-driven architecture using Layout Builder, Paragraphs, and UI Patterns.
3.4. Configuration Management Strategy - Approach to managing configurations across the multisite platform.
3.5. Third-Party Authentication
3.6. Feeds Integration for Regular Data Import.
4. Value-Added User Engagement Widgets
4.1. Development of user engagement widgets heavily relying on Drupal CORE APIs and custom JavaScript to enhance user experience (in place of going decoupled route).
5. Sites and Content Migration Strategy
5.1. Approach to reconciling sites with different tech stacks into a multisite platform.
5.2. Ensuring data consistency and handling high volumes of content (over a million records).
6. Demo of Key Features
6.1 Live demonstration of the critical features developed.
7. Outcomes
7.1 Results and outcomes achieved through the project.
7.2 Metrics and success stories demonstrating the project's impact.
items.summary: This session will delve into the comprehensive case study of the American Diabetes Association's digital landscape overhaul using Drupal 10 and multisite architecture....

items.title: Being a Drupal developer in a small third world country
items.full_description: I am from Sri Lanka and I want to talk about my efforts to promote Drupal and train developers in Sri Lanka. Also I want to talk about how the journey has been so far working as Drupal developer in Sri Lanka with a small community. How I started with Drupal 6 and went onto develop the website for the largest telecommunication provider in Sri Lanka (Sri Lanka Telecom) and from there on went on to work with The UK government as well as Gov agencies in Australia and also different brands in New Zealand and finally landed working with Pfizer UK as a Drupal Engineer in FFW / JAKALA.
items.summary: I am from Sri Lanka and I want to talk about my efforts to promote Drupal and train developers in Sri Lanka....

items.title: End-to-end observability with decoupled Drupal and microservices? Yes, it’s possible now!
items.full_description:
Monitoring and debugging a simple decoupled project with our Drupal at the backend and a separate JS frontend app is already a tricky task, right?
Let’s throw in a couple of microservices there in different languages like Python, Go, Node.js, which keep crashing, and a pretty slow and unstable third-party API… And suddenly the task became a real pain in the neck!
From my session, you will learn how to easily integrate OpenTelemetry into Drupal and all other components of your infrastructure, and get the full observability of what’s happening in your decoupled project!
You will able to see in a single interface how all the components of your infrastructure perform: all logs, metrics, end-to-end traces from Drupal, Go, Python, databases - all in one place! And all this - via opensource products on your homeserver, with no penny paid for commercial services!
And even more! For a specific failed operation, that causes an error for a user on the frontend, you can easily find and see the whole trace of it! Starting from a user click on a button in your JS app, through Drupal request, and to a backtrace of one of your microservices, that crashed.
Come to listen, and get rid of your Drupal observability pain!
items.summary: Monitoring and debugging a simple decoupled project with our Drupal at the backend and a separate JS frontend app is already a tricky task, right?...

items.title: Afternoon tea
Dance with Waterfall and Agile
items.full_description:
We, as a Drupal shop, love agile developmenmt for sure. But in many Asian countries, at least here in Japan, business people are conservative, and they love waterfall development.
We have seen many Drupal development projects go down the drain due to the contradiction between the client's waterfall mind and the developer's agile mind.
We, Digital Circus, have spent over a decade working on this problem and have developed a methodology to mix our clients' waterfall culture with our own agile approach to reach a common goal.
In this session, we will share the secrets of how an agile development company can work with waterfall clients.
items.summary: We, as a Drupal shop, love agile developmenmt for sure....

items.title: Enhancing Drupal with PHP 8: Exploring New Features and Compatibility
items.full_description:
Each version of Drupal brings in new changes. And the changes coming to Drupal Core are easier to be made, thanks to semantic versioning! It is easier to get a new experimental module/theme in core and work towards getting it stable and production ready.
While a lot of such user-facing development happens with each release, there are also a lot of under-the-hood improvements that happen as well. One of the major changes is keeping up with the releases of the dependent projects. This session focuses on PHP and its release cycle. We will talk about:
1. We will discuss in detail some exciting features of different versions of PHP 8 (8.1, 8.2, 8.3) bring and share some examples. This will include things like the JIT compiler, strict type hinting, constructor property promotion, attributes, Enums, disallowing dynamic properties for objects.
2. PHP version requirement with each version of Drupal core. E.g. For Drupal 10 the PHP version required is 8.1. Drupal 11 will require PHP 8.3
3. How Drupal core, and related projects (e.g. Symfony) keeps up with the version updates (while keeping backwards compatibility when required).
4. How can you use them in your contributed or custom code.
Intended audience — developers with some knowledge of writing custom code with PHP.
Takeaway — understanding of new exciting PHP 8 features implemented in Drupal core and how to implement them in your code.
items.summary: Each version of Drupal brings in new changes....

items.title: Defining Drupal Starshot with user-centered design
items.track: user experience, accessibility and design drupal starshot
items.full_description:
This session will give an overview of how we’ve prioritized the product definition for Starshot, which role research has played and what are the future plans.
Research and data are directing our decisions and efforts with strategies that go from product strategy definition, user personas, user flows or user testing. From the name of the product, to the feature prioritization or design tone, we’ve been intentional on where we go so the community can work together on building the right thing. At the same time we’ve been able to involve the community in this definition process and we’ll give an overview of how we’ve been able to do so.
items.summary: This session will give an overview of how we’ve prioritized the product definition for Starshot, which role research has played and what are the future plans....

items.title: Supercharge your company with an open source business model
items.full_description: Traditional digital agencies are typically reliant on subservient relationships with global technology giants, with zero influence on the software their businesses rely on. High staff turnover and low profitability are flow-on effects that makes long term sustainability for independent agencies often unattainable. However, a business that specialises in open source software can flip this traditional agency model on its head by taking control of the software they use, contributing to its evolution and building a company that's sustainable and highly profitable. 15 years after co-founding PreviousNext, Owen will share his experience of building a company with staff tenure and profitability that's more than triple the industry average whilst remaining committed to the global success of Drupal's open source software. Lessons from this session can be applied directly to your own digital agency straight away.
items.summary: Traditional digital agencies are typically reliant on subservient relationships with global technology giants, with zero influence on the software their businesses rely on....

items.title: What, Why, and When for Recipes & Distributions with Starshot
items.full_description:
As the Starshot initiative was introduced, people have started to think more about the differences and use cases between Drupal distributions and recipes. This session will cover the "what, why and when" for both distributions and recipes, and share my thought on how recipes can evolve with the help of Starshot.
Key Points:
1. What: Understanding the "what" aspect involves defining what Drupal distributions and recipes are. This includes their core functionalities, features, and how they differ from each other. By clarifying the "what," attendees can grasp the fundamental concepts behind these components.
2. Why: Exploring the "why" aspect delves into the reasons behind using Drupal distributions and recipes. This covers the benefits, advantages, and specific use cases where one might choose a distribution over a recipe or vice versa. Understanding the "why" can help dev/organization make informed decisions based on their project requirements.
3. When: Addressing the "when" aspect focuses on determining the appropriate timing for utilizing Drupal distributions and recipes. This includes identifying scenarios where one option may be more suitable than the other based on project scope, timeline, complexity, and scalability requirements. Understanding the "when" can guide dev/organization in implementing the most effective solution for them.
items.summary: As the Starshot initiative was introduced, people have started to think more about the differences and use cases between Drupal distributions and recipes....

items.title: Let's write and expand your Drupal books from your local community!
items.full_description:
In this session, Takahiro Komatsu, Drupal Meetup Toyota co-organizer, will share the experience of how we collaborated with community members in Japan to write and expand Drupal books.
This session will cover:
- The reasons why we wrote the books
- How we collaborated
- What we did before and after publishing
- What we learned and gained
- What we plan to do in the future
Target audience - people who are interested in expanding Drupal in your community.
Takeaway - understanding of key points in writing self-published books, managing projects, and technologies.
items.summary: In this session, Takahiro Komatsu, Drupal Meetup Toyota co-organizer, will share the experience of how we collaborated with community members in Japan to write and expand Drupal books....

items.title: Splash Awards
Drupal CMS Candidate Release Party
items.full_description:
Join us as we celebrate the launch of the highly anticipated Drupal CMS Release Candidate at DrupalCon Singapore!
Whether you're a long-time Drupal enthusiast or new to the community, this event is the perfect opportunity to connect with developers, designers, and thought leaders who are shaping the future of web development.
Enjoy an evening of networking, insightful discussions, and a look at what this new release means for the Drupal ecosystem. Expect great conversations, fun surprises, and a toast to the evolution of open-source innovation.
The Drupal CMS Release Party is sponsored by Previous Next, Optasy, Axelerant and Acquia.

items.title: Welcome
Drupal CMS Panel: Key Leads Answer Your Questions

Join us for an engaging Drupal CMS panel featuring key leaders from the Starshot project in a fireside chat-style discussion. They will share their perspectives on the future of Drupal, current challenges, and opportunities, while also answering questions from the audience.
Panelists:
Dries Buytaert
Lauri Timmanee
Pamela Barone
Cristina Chumillas
James Abrahams
Tim Plunkett
Gábor Hojtsy (moderator)
items.summary: Join us for an engaging Drupal CMS panel featuring key leaders from the Starshot project in a fireside chat-style discussion....

items.title: Morning Tea
Mastering UI Patterns in Drupal 10: Building Reusable and Dynamic Components
items.full_description:
I am excited to present an insightful session on "Mastering UI Patterns in Drupal 10: Building Reusable and Dynamic Components." This session aims to equip developers, designers, and site builders with the knowledge and skills needed to leverage UI Patterns effectively within the Drupal 10 ecosystem.
Overview:
1. UI Patterns in Drupal:
Introduction to UI Patterns and their significance in modern web development.
Understanding the core concepts and benefits of using UI Patterns in Drupal.
2. Building Reusable Components Using UI Patterns:
Step-by-step guide to creating reusable UI components.
Best practices for designing flexible and maintainable UI elements.
3. Integrating UI Patterns with Field Groups, Field Templates, and Views:
Demonstrating how to enhance Drupal's Field Groups and Field Templates with UI Patterns.
Exploring the seamless integration of UI Patterns with Views to create dynamic and consistent layouts.
4. The UI Patterns Ecosystem:
Overview of the tools and modules that complement UI Patterns in Drupal.
Tips for optimizing workflows and maximizing the potential of the UI Patterns ecosystem.
Why Attend?
This session is tailored to empower you with practical knowledge and hands-on experience in utilizing UI Patterns for building robust and scalable Drupal applications. Whether you are a seasoned developer or new to Drupal, you will gain valuable insights into enhancing your project's UI consistency, reusability, and maintainability.
items.summary: I am excited to present an insightful session on "Mastering UI Patterns in Drupal 10: Building Reusable and Dynamic Components." This session aims to equip developers, designers, and site builders with the knowledge and...

items.title: Building Multi-Language Sites in Drupal: Unlocking Global Potential
items.full_description:
Ever wondered how to seamlessly extend your website’s reach to a global audience without compromising on user experience or content quality? Join in as we delve into the intricacies of building robust multi-language sites in Drupal. In this session, we will explore not just the “how,” but the “why” behind multilingual setups. What makes Drupal a powerful choice for global sites? How can you manage translations efficiently while maintaining high performance and search engine visibility?
Engage in an interactive exploration of strategies and tools for setting up and managing multilingual content. We’ll cover essential configuration steps, tackle common challenges, and share tips for optimizing both user experience and technical implementation. You’ll leave with a deeper understanding of the nuances involved in creating a cohesive, multilingual experience that resonates with diverse audiences around the world.
Key Topics:
1. Multilingual Setup: Configuring Drupal for multiple languages, including site settings and language detection.
2. Content Translation: Best practices for setting up and managing content translations.
3. Localization Tools: Using modules like Locale, Content Translation, and Translation Management Tools to streamline multilingual content management.
4. Performance and SEO: Optimizing multi-language sites for performance and search engine optimization.
items.summary: Ever wondered how to seamlessly extend your website’s reach to a global audience without compromising on user experience or content quality?...

items.title: Panel: Meet the Drupal Asia Steering Committee and maybe join us?
items.full_description:
The Drupal Asia Steering Committee is a volunteer, non-profit community body that has helped bring DrupalCon Singapore to life in partnership with the Drupal Association.
As part of Linux Australia, our goal is to help promote Drupal as an open source solution across Asia and the Asia Pacific region through the execution of more DrupalCons in Asia and by fostering local community bodies and helping them to succeed.
In this panel, the 6 Steering Committee members will discuss the future of our mission and how you can get involved, and of course we'll answer your questions about our work and our future as a combined community.
items.summary: The Drupal Asia Steering Committee is a volunteer, non-profit community body that has helped bring DrupalCon Singapore to life in partnership with the Drupal Association....

items.title: Navigating Tomorrow: The Future of Websites in the Age of AI and Content Proliferation
items.full_description:
The landscape of websites is undergoing a seismic shift, driven by an insatiable demand for new content and the impending influence of artificial intelligence. In this session, we embark on a journey into the heart of "The Future of Websites," exploring the dynamic interplay between escalating content needs and the transformative role of AI in shaping the digital ecosystem.
Join us for a forward-thinking session where we dissect the symbiotic relationship between content demand, artificial intelligence, and the evolution of websites. As the story unfolds, be prepared to glean insights that will empower you to navigate and lead in the dynamic future of website development.
Potential Topics for Discussion:
Content Proliferation:
Uncover the driving forces behind the unprecedented demand for content in today's digital era.
Review strategies to meet the content explosion, ensuring websites remain not only relevant but also thrive in a content-saturated environment.
AI's Impact on the Ecosystem:
Delve into the imminent influence of artificial intelligence on website functionality and user experience.
Explore real-world applications of AI in content creation, personalization, and adaptive user interfaces.
The Unfolding Narrative:
Peer into the future as we paint a compelling picture of what lies ahead for websites.
Discuss anticipated trends, challenges, and opportunities that will define the narrative of website development and management.
items.summary: The landscape of websites is undergoing a seismic shift, driven by an insatiable demand for new content and the impending influence of artificial intelligence....

items.title: Mutual Benefits: How Government Adoption of Drupal Spurs Local Communities and Unlocks Synergy
items.full_description:
When it comes to government websites, Drupal is everywhere. In 2021, around 56% of the world’s government websites relied on Drupal. Numerous case studies illustrate the profound technical benefits of choosing Drupal. Serving both national governments and local administrative divisions, Drupal has a proven track record as a reliable, cost-efficient framework for websites of varying scales.
What is often overlooked, however, is that when a government commits to Drupal, it sets off a virtuous cycle that can significantly bolster the local Drupal ecosystem. If coordinated well, this may unlock further cost reductions for government bodies and accelerate innovation for governments, vendors, and developers alike.
In this session, we will explore how government adoption of Drupal acts as a catalyst for local economic and community development. We will explain how governments' decisions to commit to Drupal can create opportunities for local vendors, who in turn provide jobs and foster growth for local Drupal developers. This dynamic enables local developers to organize events, share knowledge, and boost community engagement.
Moreover, we will discuss the immense advantages when governments and local Drupal communities join forces to unlock the full benefits of open source. By sharing code, experiences and innovations, governments can achieve substantial cost savings while promoting transparency and efficiency. This collaboration nurtures a thriving ecosystem of developers who are continuously learning and giving back to the Drupal community. The LocalGov Drupal initiative in the UK is a shining example of the synergy that arises when local governments and the open-source community combine strengths.
Join us to discover why government adoption of Drupal is not just a technical or financial decision but a strategic move that can lead to thriving local Drupal communities. We will explain how to maximize these benefits for both governmental bodies and local developers, and illustrate the synergy that arises when governments commit to an open-source project like Drupal.
items.summary: When it comes to government websites, Drupal is everywhere....

items.title: First Time Contributor Workshop
items.full_description:
Did you know that Drupal (the software) is built by people just like you and me? If you haven't contributed yet (or need a refresher), this is your opportunity to learn how to contribute, through code, documentation, project managing, marketing and more!
Those who are new to the process and tools of contributing to Drupal, you are invited to attend this “First-Time Contributor Workshops”. Get up to speed with community tools: Drupal.org, issue queues, communication channels, and using cloud development tool options. Note you don't need to be a developer or use development tools to contribute to the project.
items.summary: Did you know that Drupal (the software) is built by people just like you and me?...

items.title: Innovation and the future of Drupal
items.full_description:
The Drupal project thrives on innovation and collaboration, shaping the future of digital experiences. This roundtable, moderated by Alex Moreno, brings together a diverse group of Drupal experts and community leaders to explore critical questions about where Drupal stands today and where it is heading.
Joining Alex are:
- Alejandro Moreno Lopez, a thought leader in digital transformation and open-source technologies.
- Lenny Moskalyk, an experienced Drupal practitioner and advocate for fostering community-driven contributions.
- Cristina Chumillas, a core contributor and advocate for design systems and user experience in Drupal.
- Hemant Gupta, an expert in bridging technology and business needs through Drupal.
- Piyuesh Kumar, an experienced Drupal evangelist and community builder.
This session will dive into key topics, including:
- The past, present, and future of Drupal—reflecting on its journey so far.
- Exploring whether Drupal is still innovative and whether recent efforts are achieving their goals.
- Addressing the challenge of growth: how can we attract new contributors and users, and should the focus be on new talent or seasoned professionals?
- Speculating on the future of Drupal, with each panelist sharing their vision for the road ahead.
Whether you’re a developer, strategist, or enthusiast, this roundtable will offer a variety of perspectives and insights into Drupal’s current trajectory and its potential for innovation and growth.
items.summary: The Drupal project thrives on innovation and collaboration, shaping the future of digital experiences....

Accelerating Web Performance: The Power of Fastly and Drupal Integration
items.full_description:
As the digital landscape evolves, performance and collaboration are key to
delivering fast, reliable, and scalable web experiences. Bring your lunch and
join us as we explore how the integration of Fastly's advanced CDN and edge
cloud platform with Drupal is transforming website performance, scalability,

Discover how Fastly improves load times, reduces server load, and enhances

items.title: Consistency and Collaboration: The Future of Front-End Development with Design Tokens
items.full_description:
Have you ever been on a project where the design process was slowed down by repetitive tasks such as manually updating colors, fonts, and spacing? Discover a forward-thinking approach to enriching user experiences by integrating design tokens with Storybook and Figma in Drupal. This session explores how design tokens serve as a central repository for design decisions, ensuring consistent and scalable outcomes across complex projects.
Explore how Storybook’s interactive development environment fosters seamless collaboration between designers and developers, bridging the gap between design conception and execution with precision.
We will explore the implementation of design tokens, covering best practices and practical tips to help you get started.
Outline:
1. Consistency Across Projects: Establish a uniform design language with design tokens as the authoritative source.
2. Enhanced Collaboration: Foster improved communication and collaboration between designers and developers using Storybook's interactive features and Figma Components and variables.
3. Scalability: Scale design systems effectively across large projects while maintaining coherence and minimizing redundancy.
4. Efficiency: Streamline development efforts through reusable components, reducing time and resource expenditures.
5. Adaptability: Quickly respond to design changes and project requirements with a flexible and modular approach.
6. Improved Design System Management: Simplify maintenance and updates of design systems to ensure they remain current and effective.
Learning objectives:
1. This session will illuminate how design tokens establish a definitive source for design decisions, guaranteeing coherence and scalability across expansive projects.
2. Learn how Storybook's interactive development environment facilitates effortless teamwork between designers and developers, minimizing discrepancies in design execution.
Pre-requisites:
1. Basic knowledge of component based theming.
2. Basic Knowledge of figma.
items.summary: Have you ever been on a project where the design process was slowed down by repetitive tasks such as manually updating colors, fonts, and spacing?...

items.title: From 'them' to 'us': Demystifying Drupal contribution
items.full_description:
Millions of people use Drupal, but a small fraction of those contribute in a given year. That's enough to make Drupal one of the biggest open-source projects in the world, but it still leaves a high proportion of takers to makers.
Contributing to a project the size of Drupal can be overwhelming, and some who want to contribute struggle to find a way. But we need new contributors in order to ship new versions and releases on time.
In this session we'll examine the contribution ecosystem, from the perspective of two members of the core committer team: pameeela (Pamela Barone) and larowlan (Lee Rowlands).
They will share:
* some of their background and various skillsets
* how they came to become part of the committer team
* insights into how Drupal is actually built
The session will cover topics such as the issue queue, community channels, roles and processes that keep the project moving towards it goals.
If you're new to Drupal, this session might help you find your niche and be your impetus for getting more involved in this amazing community. If you've been around a long time, you might find answers to questions you've always wanted to ask but never did.
The session will be delivered in two parts, the first part with the two/three presenters sharing their insights, and the second part open the floor to the audience to ask their questions.
items.summary: Millions of people use Drupal, but a small fraction of those contribute in a given year....

items.title: Using Drupal’s AI module to power your Drupal websites and potential plans for Starshot.
items.full_description:
Description
When ChatGPT first launched, along with many other people I thought my job could be over! Instead, we as Drupal developers have the potential to become indispensable through offering our clients valuable and effective AI-powered solutions and powerful AI applications.
Enter the AI module. A small group from the Drupal community have created a foundational AI module that gives you all the building blocks you need to build powerful AI applications. It is built on top of an AI Abstraction layer, meaning it can be used with any model you want, including open source self hosted models.
This talk will cover what is in the AI module and how to use it and will be useful for both site builders and developers alike. Some powerful Drupal tools made possible with our AI module include:
- Creating whole Drupal sites by merely showing a design.
- Creating content types, content and even views by uploading PDFs full of content.
- A customer service bot that can read your own technical manuals.
- Creating a webform from your rough hand-drawn sketch, which you can then edit by chatting to AI!
The AI module has a number of sub-modules that give site builders the building blocks to create these applications themselves.
- AI Search: Index your content so AI can access it and understand the meaning of potentially huge libraries of documents.
- AI Automators: Chain prompts together and create the complex applications all in Drupal via the familiar fields UI. Expand them to Agents to allow them to behave in an autonomous manner and decide how to approach a problem themselves.
- AI in Starshot: Work in Progress - This will be shaped by discussions with the community. Initial ideas include AI creating content for you, helping you in the content editing experience or even creating whole Drupal sites and Recipes for you from scratch!
I will provide a surface-level demo of the modules in action, followed by a deep dive into how they work to help the audience understand the potential applications of using Drupal.We will then explore the possible future applications and developments.
(Note: The concepts behind AI Search are also known as RAG (Retrieval Augmented Generation), Embeddings and Vector Search.
Module found at https://www.drupal.org/project/ai
items.summary: Description When ChatGPT first launched, along with many other people I thought my job could be over!...

items.title: Improved authoring experience with layout builder: balancing contrib and custom modules
items.full_description:
It's widely known that Drupal's content authoring experience has room for improvement, particularly in comparison to platforms like Wix and Wordpress. Over the years, there have been improvements by both core and contrib modules: CKEditor, paragraphs, layout builder, Gutenberg, and any of the combinations.
In this session, we will first go through the available solutions to date, from core layout builder, to contrib layout paragraphs, to custom DXP like Dropsolid Rocketship or Acquia Site Studio. We will then share our observation of our clients' needs, and explore the solution our team in Pixel Onion has been using for our recent projects, which leverages on core layout builder, and contributed modules like Gin theme, layout builder paragraphs, layout builder base and some custom glue code.
One client stated that the solution has reduced the time taken to create a page from 1–2 days to merely 30 minutes!
This session will present alternatives to site builders and developers who want to provide better authoring experience for their clients. Custom glue code is kept at minimal, but pointers are given for those who want to explore advanced tweaking.
items.summary: It's widely known that Drupal's content authoring experience has room for improvement, particularly in comparison to platforms like Wix and Wordpress....

items.title: Deep Telemetry with eBPF and Rust
items.full_description:
Performance profiling is the process of reviewing and debugging applications for bottlenecks and inefficiencies to identify areas for improvement.
To profile, we utilise tools that provide us with deep telemetry data to point to these areas of improvement. The golden standard for these tools is paid subscriptions e.g. New Relic and Backfire.io. On the open source side, popular tools such as Xdebug and SPX are great but lack the features needed for profiling in production.
Open source options are outclassed by paid subscription-based services because of the effort and deep technical knowledge required to implement them. But there is hope...
eBPF (Extended Berkeley Packet Filter) lets programmers load and execute lightweight programs within the Linux kernel. This is a big deal because it enables us to easily observe and instrument our applications in ways that were incredibly difficult to do in the past.
This talk outlines the journey we have undertaken to build an open-source telemetry system on top of eBPF, Rust, and Go for instrumenting the PHP applications which run on our hosting platform, Skpr.
In this talk, I will cover the following topics:
- The current state of monitoring and tracing
- What is eBPF?
- How to capture tracing data with eBPF
- What is PHP's Observer API?
- How to write a PECL extension with Rust
- The decisions we made when designing our tracing implementation
items.summary: Performance profiling is the process of reviewing and debugging applications for bottlenecks and inefficiencies to identify areas for improvement....

items.title: The Drupal.org Engineering team: How we're building a new Drupal.org, contributor tools, and support
items.full_description:
Join the Drupal.org engineering team for updates on our recent work. You'll learn how the engineering team has worked with community partners to build out new.drupal.org, a fresh face for the home of the community. You'll get insight into how we're preparing to migrate Drupal.org issues into GitLab. You'll get an update on many of the ways that the Drupal Association supports the Starshot Initiative - including coordinating partner sponsorship of tracks and documentation, building key functionality like secure signing and the project browser api, and more.
We'll talk about all of this and more, as well as take your questions about the role of the Drupal Association engineering team in the project, and what's on the horizon for the future.
items.summary: Join the Drupal.org engineering team for updates on our recent work....

items.title: Afternoon Tea
Master the Multilingual Maze: Harnessing Drupal to Achieve Global Success
items.full_description:
In the competitive e-bike industry, Tern manages nearly 40 language variants on its global branding website, leveraging Drupal for seamless localization and content management. This session is tailored for content managers, digital strategists, and marketers aiming to enhance multilingual content workflows and optimize digital marketing.
We’ll explore how Tern’s website supports global branding and marketing goals, the role of localization in delivering contextually relevant content, and strategies for tailoring campaigns to diverse audiences. Attendees will also learn practical approaches to managing translations, handling content across multiple languages, and using AI tools to improve efficiency.
In this talk, I will cover the following topics:
- Localization Matters
Why translations vary by country and context, and how to deliver the most relevant content.
- Connecting with Global Audiences
Adapting campaigns for diverse audiences, gracefully suggesting languages, and addressing missing translations.
- Content Management Strategies
Updating translations, decoupling content creation for campaigns, and leveraging AI tools for efficiency.
items.summary: In the competitive e-bike industry, Tern manages nearly 40 language variants on its global branding website, leveraging Drupal for seamless localization and content management....

items.title: Maximizing Potential: Our Journey with Drupal Internships at QED42
items.full_description:
In this session, we will delve into the strategic benefits and challenges of implementing a Drupal internship program at an organizational level. Drawing from our extensive experiences at QED42, we will cover the entire lifecycle of the internship program, from initial requirements and sourcing of interns to the hiring process, comprehensive training, and final evaluation with potential for full-time employment.
We will explore the following key phases of a successful Drupal internship program:
1. Organizational Requirements: Identifying the needs and expectations from the internship program.
2. Sourcing Interns: Effective strategies for attracting potential interns in a landscape where Drupal is less popular among students.
3. Hiring Process: Best practices for selecting the right candidates who have the potential to thrive in a Drupal environment.
4. Training Process: Structuring a robust training program that addresses the steep learning curve of Drupal, ensuring interns gain the necessary skills and knowledge efficiently.
5. Evaluation and Hiring: Implementing a fair and thorough evaluation process that can lead to full-time hiring, converting interns into productive team members.
Given Drupal's complexity and its declining popularity among students, attracting and nurturing new talent is increasingly challenging. This session aims to provide actionable insights and share proven methodologies to overcome these challenges, ultimately enabling organizations to build a strong, capable Drupal team from the ground up.
Benefits of Attending This Session:
1. From Drupal's Perspective: By highlighting the significance of internships, we can introduce fresh minds to Drupal, ensuring the growth and sustainability of the Drupal community. This session will showcase how internships can serve as a vital pipeline for new talent and innovation.
2. From the Organization's Perspective: Organizations will learn how to effectively generate new Drupal talent tailored to their specific needs. This session will provide strategies to overcome the hurdles of hiring and training in a niche field, leading to a stronger and more dynamic team.
3. From the Individual's Perspective: For individuals, particularly students and career switchers, this session will demonstrate how a Drupal internship can be a significant career-building opportunity. Attendees will understand how to leverage internships to gain valuable skills, experience, and potentially secure full-time positions in the Drupal ecosystem.
Insights from QED42:
Throughout the session, we at QED42 will be sharing our insights and the lessons learned over several years of conducting our successful Drupal internship program. We will discuss the challenges we faced, the strategies we employed to overcome them, and the significant positive outcomes we achieved.
One of the key successes of our program has been the transition of interns to full-time roles where they have taken on key responsibilities within our organization. These individuals have grown to become vital members of our team, contributing to our capabilities and driving our projects forward.
Join us to learn how to effectively design, implement, and optimize a Drupal internship program that not only benefits the interns but also significantly contributes to your organization's growth and success.
items.summary: In this session, we will delve into the strategic benefits and challenges of implementing a Drupal internship program at an organizational level....

items.title: A Drupal core maintainer shares peer code review best practices
items.full_description:
Downloadable slides for this session - https://bit.ly/xjm-singapore-slides
Improve your teams' peer code review process, your code maintainability, and your Drupal contributions. Learn from a Drupal core release manager how to give an efficient and effective peer code review.
Peer code reviews are essential, improving maintainability, increasing productivity, and reducing costs. They can also reduce the rate of defects (bugs) introduced into a codebase by as much as 90 percent. Most importantly, they build a shared understanding of the application within a team and are an opportunity for developers to mentor each other.
Drupal core's peer code review process has two phases:
1. An initial peer code review cycle with one or more other contributors
2. A final stage of code review by a Drupal core committer (one of the few like xjm who can accept changes into Drupal core's production codebase).
As a result, the core committers are exposed to all the peer review (good and not-so-good) throughout the Drupal core issue queue. This makes committers ideal mentors and subject matter experts on both best practices and pitfalls to avoid.
In this session, you will also discover research and psychological principles on effective code review. You will learn how structuring your change sets for the reviewer can improve the whole team's code and maintainability. Finally, you will learn about automation and tooling that can help reviewers focus on what's most important.
items.summary: Downloadable slides for this session - https://bit.ly/xjm-singapore-slides Improve your teams' peer code review process, your code maintainability, and your Drupal contributions....

items.title: How to setup a disaster recovery of highly critical drupal sites in AWS
items.full_description: This presentation will present and setup a disaster recovery for highly critical drupal site with standard RPO and RTO in AWS. Will demo a DR exercise on how to respond and mitigate an infrastructure/application downtime issue. Technology used: Drupal 10, AWS EKS, RDS, Secret Manager, Secret Store CSI, S3

items.title: Enhancing Drupal SEO with Real-Time Updates: Integrating Yoast SEO and Site Studio
items.full_description:
While the existing Drupal search engine optimization (SEO) module provides
comprehensive SEO tools directly within the Drupal content management system, this
can be optimized by integrating Yoast SEO and Site Studio for further performance
enhancement. This module extends SEO capabilities by integrating real-time updates
and enhancing user interactions with SEO metadata within the Drupal environment.
In the session, Immaculate Xavier, technical lead at Material, explores how integrating
Yoast SEO and Site Studio not only improves SEO efficiency but also enhances the
overall content management experience for Drupal users. In this session, Immaculate
will:
- Explore the key features of Yoast SEO model
- Evaluate the limitations of the current SEO model
- Present the benefits of using your developed Yoast SEO integration module,
including real-time content synchronization, enhanced usability, scalability and
flexibility
- Discuss further advancements with Site Studio components in Drupal
items.summary: While the existing Drupal search engine optimization (SEO) module provides comprehensive SEO tools directly within the Drupal content management system, this can be optimized by integrating Yoast SEO and Site Studio for...

items.title: Experience Builder Initiative
items.full_description:
Join us to hear the latest news on the Experience Builder initiative from the initiative leaders. The Experience Builder module will enable site builders without Drupal experience to easily theme and build their entire website using only their browser, without the need to write code beyond basic HTML, CSS, and templating markup (e.g., Twig). It will also enable content creators to create and compose content on any part of the page without relying on developers.
We will provide an update on the status and progress of the initiative. We'll also walk through our plan, provide a clear idea on what changes to expect, where are we, and where we need help.
In this session you'll:
- Learn what are the problems we're trying to solve within this initiative
- Get a sense of how we're planning to tackle there problems
- Explore upcoming opportunities for involvement
items.summary: Join us to hear the latest news on the Experience Builder initiative from the initiative leaders....
